id: "19.2.36"
title: "oVirt / Red Hat Virtualization (RHV) Storage Domain Used Percent and Low-Space Warning"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.36 Â· oVirt / Red Hat Virtualization (RHV) Storage Domain Used Percent and Low-Space Warning

## Description

Monitors oVirt and Red Hat Virtualization (RHV) data-storage domains with engine-observed `available` / `used` / `committed` byte math, `external_status` from the management plane, the percent-based low-space warning (codes such as 983) and the absolute critical-free gate that blocks new disks (such as 984), and optional ownership rows from a lookup so tenant-specific watermarks and change freezes are respected. This is the engineâ€™s view of capacity (including thin-provisioned committed), not a bare NFS or LUN `df` on the back end, by design â€” that distinction is what blocks surprise lockouts on thin domains.

## Value

Storage-domain exhaustion stops disk creation, snapshot merge, and template imports at the point customers feel it. Surfacing the engineâ€™s own low-space and critical-free thresholds, committed oversubscription, and operational `external_status` before those operations fail keeps provisioning and backup runbooks on schedule, lets capacity owners act during business hours, and gives operations a single, comparable signal across NFS, iSCSI, FCP, GlusterFS, and POSIX-backed domains.

## Implementation

Build the three HEC pollers, ship JSON with `Accept: application/json` from the oVirt Engine REST API, add `ovirt_storage_domain_owner.csv` and `default/transforms.conf` for the lookup, parse events so `code` 983/984 and `storage_domain_id` are joinable, then schedule the provided SPL every five minutes. Wire alerts so critical-space and `external_status=error` do not get muted by a change-freeze row in the lookup. Keep optional engine and vdsm file logs in separate panels for post-mortems, not the primary KPI math.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

oVirt is the open-source enterprise virtualization management stack (KVM hypervisors, engine API, and optional self-hosted engine). Red Hat Virtualization (RHV) was the commercial oVirt family; Red Hat announced RHV sunset for new deployments, yet many production estates remain on RHV 4.4 with extended support while upstream oVirt continues. Storage domains: data domains store VM and template disks; ISO domains hold installation media; export domains (where still used) support import/export. Each data center has a master data domain; without a working master, new workload placement and disk operations in that data center fail even if capacity exists elsewhere. Backing types in scope are NFS, iSCSI, FCP, GlusterFS, and POSIX. Gluster is the deprecated oVirt+Gluster â€śhyperconvergedâ€ť path but remains common in long-lived fields â€” treat it with extra brick-level checks, not with this engine-only math alone. Why you cannot just watch `df` on the export: the engine enforces `used`, `available`, and `committed` (declared size sum) and applies `warning_low_space_indicator` as a free-percent floor and `critical_space_action_blocker` as an absolute free-gigabyte floor that blocks new disk operations â€” those rules are not the same as â€ś10% free on the filerâ€ť when thin provisioning, snapshots, and oversubscription are in play. The engine can show `external_status=error` when the service layer, SPM election, or host connectivity is bad while a storage array still answers pings â€” the UC tracks that operational flag alongside bytes. The percent watermark and the critical GB watermark are different controls: both need to be true inputs to automation and dashboards, not one or the other. The lookup `ovirt_storage_domain_owner.csv` (transforms stanza `ovirt_storage_domain_owner`) carries: `data_center` (string), `storage_domain_name` (string), `min_free_pct` (overrides the engine default for planning when you need a stricter or looser SLO), `expected_critical_action_blocker_gb` (validates the engine or documents what you think `engine-config` should be), `business_service` (ITSM routing), `owner_email` (on-call), `change_freeze_until` (ISO-8601 date or date+time, UTC in ops teams), `expansion_planned_date` (optional visibility on funded work), and `criticality_class` (A/B/C for routing weight). The lookup join key is the pair `(data_center, storage_domain_name)`; normalize names the same way the poller does or joins will miss.

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection

Authentication: obtain a short-lived `Bearer` from `POST` to `https://<engine>:443/ovirt-engine/sso/oauth/token` with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded` and a body like `grant_type=password&username=admin%40internal&password=<value>&scope=ovirt-app-api` for interactive SSO, or a dedicated app user with the Application scope on oVirt 4.5+; handle `401` by refreshing the token, `503` with exponential back-off (engine in failover), and never log secrets. Always send `Accept: application/json` â€” the same paths default to XML and Splunk will not parse the response as the JSON the props stanza expects. A minimal stdlib HEC poller (Python 3, `urllib.request`, `ssl.create_default_context` plus `load_verify_locations` for a PEM bundle) should read `OVIRT_ENGINE`, `OVIRT_USER`, `OVIRT_PASSWORD` or a refresh token, request `/ovirt-engine/api/storagedomains` and `/ovirt-engine/api/hosts` on a 300s `interval` for the capacity and host context feeds, and `/ovirt-engine/api/events` (parameter `max=200` with a time-bounded or code-filtered `search` where your version supports it) on a 60s interval so code 984-class conditions surface before the next five-minute block poll. Map each HEC `event` JSON with explicit `sourcetype` values: `ovirt:engine:storagedomain`, `ovirt:engine:host`, and `ovirt:engine:event`. In `inputs.conf` on a Heavy Forwarder, wire three stanzas or one driver with internal scheduling â€” example:

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ovirt_hec/bin/ovirt_poll.py storagedomains]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
sourcetype = ovirt:engine:storagedomain
source = ovirt:api:storagedomains
index = virtualization
disabled = 0
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ovirt_hec/bin/ovirt_poll.py events]
python.version = python3
interval = 60
sourcetype = ovirt:engine:event
source = ovirt:api:events
index = virtualization
disabled = 0
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ovirt_hec/bin/ovirt_poll.py hosts]
python.version = python3
interval = 300
sourcetype = ovirt:engine:host
source = ovirt:api:hosts
index = virtualization
disabled = 0
```

`props.conf` (Indexer and Search time):

```ini
[ovirt:engine:storagedomain]
KV_MODE = none
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([
]+)
TIME_PREFIX = "poll_utc":\s*"
TZ = UTC
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
[ovirt:engine:host]
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
[ovirt:engine:event]
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TZ = UTC
[ovirt:engine:log]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = true
BREAK_ONLY_BEFORE = ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}[,|\.]
MAX_EVENTS = 20000
[ovirt:vdsm:log]
TRUNCATE = 0
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = true
BREAK_ONLY_BEFORE = ^(INFO|WARN|ERROR|DEBUG)
```

`transforms.conf` for ownership:

```ini
[ovirt_storage_domain_owner]
filename = ovirt_storage_domain_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
min_matches = 0
```

`inputs.conf` (optional engine log) with Splunk_TA_nix on the engine VM:

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/ovirt-engine/engine.log]
disabled = 0
sourcetype = ovirt:engine:log
index = virtualization
```

`inputs.conf` (optional vdsm) on each hypervisor:

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log]
disabled = 0
sourcetype = ovirt:vdsm:log
index = virtualization
```

Python outline (stdlib only) â€” the live script must set `id`, `name`, `data_center`, `available`, `used`, `committed`, `external_status`, `master`, and `storage.type` from the `storagedomains` collection, and add `poll_utc` in ISO-8601 for time parsing. For events, the poller should copy `code`, `id`, the storage-domain UUID from any `storage` or `data` sub-object your engine build exposes, and the human description. If the REST `events` object omits a flat UUID, `rex` the href `storagedomains/{uuid}` and emit `storage_domain_id` for joins.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert

Canonical SPL (align `spl` in this use case, saved search name, and alert actions):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.36 â€” oVirt / RHV storage domain capacity: free %, low_space and critical_space watermarks, external_status, events 983/984. Multisearch ovirt:engine:storagedomain + host + event; lookup ovirt_storage_domain_owner; coalesce camel/snake; streamstats growth â†’ eta_to_critical_h; five-tier severity with freeze demotion that preserves critical.")`
| multisearch [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:storagedomain" earliest=-2h@h | comment("UC-19.2.36 â€” oVirt / RHV storage domain free %, watermarks, external_status, events 983+984. Primary: ovirt:engine:storagedomain. Then filter to storagedomain; events joined by storage_domain_id.") ] [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:host" earliest=-2h@h ] [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:event" earliest=-2h@h | eval _code=tonumber(toString(coalesce(code, eventCode, "")), 10) | where _code=983 OR _code=984 ]
| where sourcetype="ovirt:engine:storagedomain"
| eval data_center=toString(coalesce(data_center, dataCenter, data_center_name, dataCenterName, dc, ""))
| eval storage_domain_id=toString(coalesce(id, storage_domain_id, storageDomainId, "") )
| eval storage_domain_name=toString(coalesce(name, storage_domain_name, storageDomainName, ""))
| eval storage_type=toString(coalesce(storage_type, storageType, st_type, "unknown"))
| eval master=if((lower(toString(coalesce(master, isMaster, "false")))="true") OR (toString(coalesce(master, isMaster, "0"))="1"), 1, 0)
| eval external_status=lower(toString(coalesce(external_status, externalStatus, "unknown")))
| eval available=tonumber(toString(coalesce(available, availableSize, freeBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval used=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used, usedSize, usedBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval committed=tonumber(toString(coalesce(committed, committedSize,"0")), 10)
| eval warning_low_space_indicator=tonumber(toString(coalesce(warning_low_space_indicator, warningLowSpaceIndicator, "10")), 10)
| eval critical_space_action_blocker=tonumber(toString(coalesce(critical_space_action_blocker, criticalSpaceActionBlocker, "5")), 10)
| eval total_bytes=if((isnotnull(available)) AND (isnotnull(used)) AND ((available+used)>0), available+used, 0)
| eval free_pct=if((total_bytes>0), round(((available*1.0)/total_bytes)*100, 2), 0)
| eval available_gb=round(available/1073741824, 2)
| eval committed_gb=round(committed/1073741824, 2)
| eval over_sub_ratio=if((total_bytes>0), round((committed*1.0)/total_bytes, 3), 0)
| eval crit_bytes=if((isnotnull(critical_space_action_blocker)) AND (critical_space_action_blocker>0), critical_space_action_blocker*1073741824, 5368709120)
| sort 0 + _time, storage_domain_id
| streamstats current=f last(available) as avail_p last(_time) as t_p by storage_domain_id
| eval depl_bps=if((isnotnull(avail_p)) AND (isnotnull(t_p)) AND (_time>t_p) AND (avail_p>available), (avail_p-available)/(_time-t_p+0.0001), 0)
| eval eta_to_critical_h=if((depl_bps>0) AND (available>crit_bytes) AND (available>0), round(((available-crit_bytes)/depl_bps)/3600, 1), null())
| stats max(_time) as _time latest(data_center) as data_center latest(storage_domain_id) as storage_domain_id latest(storage_domain_name) as storage_domain_name latest(storage_type) as storage_type latest(master) as master latest(external_status) as external_status latest(available) as available latest(used) as used latest(committed) as committed latest(warning_low_space_indicator) as warning_low_space_indicator latest(critical_space_action_blocker) as critical_space_action_blocker latest(total_bytes) as total_bytes latest(free_pct) as free_pct latest(available_gb) as available_gb latest(committed_gb) as committed_gb latest(over_sub_ratio) as over_sub_ratio latest(eta_to_critical_h) as eta_to_critical_h latest(depl_bps) as depl_bps by data_center, storage_domain_id, storage_domain_name
| eval total_bytes=if((isnotnull(available)) AND (isnotnull(used)) AND ((available+used)>0), available+used, 0)
| eval free_pct=if((total_bytes>0), round(((available*1.0)/total_bytes)*100, 2), 0)
| eval available_gb=round(available/1073741824, 2)
| eval committed_gb=round(committed/1073741824, 2)
| eval over_sub_ratio=if((total_bytes>0), round((committed*1.0)/total_bytes, 3), 0)
| eval crit_bytes=if((isnotnull(critical_space_action_blocker)) AND (critical_space_action_blocker>0), critical_space_action_blocker*1073741824, 5368709120)
| lookup ovirt_storage_domain_owner data_center storage_domain_name OUTPUT min_free_pct, expected_critical_action_blocker_gb, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, expansion_planned_date, criticality_class
| eval min_free_pct=tonumber(toString(min_free_pct), 10)
| eval expect_crit_gb=tonumber(toString(expected_critical_action_blocker_gb), 10)
| eval warning_low_space_indicator=if((isnotnull(min_free_pct)) AND (min_free_pct>0), min_free_pct, warning_low_space_indicator)
| eval critical_space_action_blocker=if((isnotnull(expect_crit_gb)) AND (expect_crit_gb>0), expect_crit_gb, critical_space_action_blocker)
| eval low_space_breach=if(free_pct<warning_low_space_indicator, 1, 0)
| eval critical_space_breach=if(available_gb<critical_space_action_blocker, 1, 0)
| join type=left storage_domain_id [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:event" earliest=-2h@h | eval _c=tonumber(toString(coalesce(code, eventCode, "")), 10) | where _c=983 OR _c=984 | eval storage_domain_id=toString(coalesce(related_storage_id, relatedStorageId, storage_domain_id, storageDomainId, storageId, id, "")) | stats max(if(_c=984,1,0)) as ev_984 max(if(_c=983,1,0)) as ev_983 by storage_domain_id ] | fillnull value=0 ev_984 ev_983
| eval recent_984=if(ev_984>0,1,0) | eval recent_983=if(ev_983>0,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((external_status="error") OR (critical_space_breach=1) OR (recent_984=1), "critical", (low_space_breach=1) OR (external_status="warning") OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_critical_h)) AND (eta_to_critical_h<24) AND (depl_bps>0)) OR (recent_983=1), "high", (free_pct<25) OR (over_sub_ratio>2.0), "medium", (free_pct<40), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval _hard=(critical_space_breach=1) OR (external_status="error") OR (recent_984=1)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (_hard=0) AND ((severity_bucket="high") OR (severity_bucket="medium") OR (severity_bucket="low")), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical", 1, severity_bucket="high", 2, severity_bucket="medium", 3, severity_bucket="low", 4, 1=1, 5)
| sort 0 sev_order, -free_pct
| table data_center, storage_domain_name, storage_type, master, external_status, available_gb, total_bytes, free_pct, committed_gb, over_sub_ratio, warning_low_space_indicator, critical_space_action_blocker, low_space_breach, critical_space_breach, eta_to_critical_h, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, expansion_planned_date, criticality_class
```

Search walkthrough: the `multisearch` performs one unioned pull so schedulers, index-time routing, and sourcetype ACL tests stay consistent across all three pollers, then the `where` keeps the numeric capacity math on the storagedomain side only. `coalesce()` chains absorb camelCase, snake_case, and poller-specific aliases. `streamstats` with `current=f` looks at the prior sample per `storage_domain_id` and builds a depletion rate in bytes per second, which feeds an ETA in hours to the free-byte floor implied by `critical_space_action_blocker`; the `stats` command keeps `latest(depl_bps)` alongside the other `latest()` fields so the `high` severity branch can still combine `eta_to_critical_h<24` with `depl_bps>0` after you collapse multiple polls per domain. The `lookup` enriches human ownership and can tighten or relax the free-percent floor via `min_free_pct` and the expected GB block via `expected_critical_action_blocker_gb` before the breach flags. The `join` to events looks for 983/984 in the lookback. `_hard` encodes the freeze override: you may demote `high` / `medium` / `low` to `ok` when a change freeze is active, but you never demote when `critical_space_breach=1`, `external_status=error`, or a 984 flag is set â€” those stay on the operations bridge until cleared.

Step 3 â€” Validate
1. In the oVirt Web Admin Portal: Storage then Storage Domains, open a domain, compare Used/Available/Committed to the most recent HEC line for the same UUID; open Events and filter oVirt messages for 983/984 in the lookback. 2. On the engine host, run `engine-config -g FreeSpaceLow` / `engine-config -g FreeSpaceCritical` (RHV/oVirt naming varies) or the documented `StorageDomainPercentageWarning` and `StorageDomainSizeGBCritical` (exact keys depend on your 4.4/4.5 build â€” cross-check the Administration Guide) so UI thresholds, API fields, and engine-config line up. 3. From a jumphost, `curl -k -H "Accept: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" "https://<engine>/ovirt-engine/api/storagedomains"` and spot-check a domain object. 4. On the back end, verify NFS, FC, or Gluster: `df -h` on the export, `multipath -ll` for FC, `gluster volume status` for Gluster â€” the engine is allowed to disagree slightly because of cache, oversubscription, and snapshot life cycles. 5. If UI and SPL differ, suspect engine cache, snapshot deletion in progress, or a thin domain whose committed sum grew faster than physical free, not only bad math.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize

Dashboard rows: (1) two single values â€” `sum(eval(critical_space_breach=1))` and `sum(eval(external_status="error"))` across the table; (2) sortable table of `free_pct` ascending; (3) scatter `over_sub_ratio` vs `free_pct`; (4) timechart of `min(free_pct)` or `min(available_gb)` by `storage_domain_name` over 7d; (5) table of `ovirt:engine:event` for codes 983/984. Alert routing: PagerDuty when `critical_space_breach=1` OR `external_status=error` OR a 984-class row in the event join; ServiceNow P2 when `low_space_breach=1` without a hard breach; Slack for `severity_bucket=medium`; email-only digest for `low`. Throttle on `(data_center, storage_domain_id)` for non-critical. Optional Splunk ITSI: map KPIs per `data_center` for critical breach count, low breach count, and worst-case `over_sub_ratio` on a 15-minute cycle. Runbooks: low_space â€” extend the NFS share or add LUNs, then rescan; critical_space_breach â€” reclaim disks, delete stale snapshots, or expand; `external_status=error` â€” verify SPM, `engine.log` for SPM moves, and `vdsm` on the current SPM host. Link optional `ovirt:engine:log` and `ovirt:vdsm:log` for deep dives.

Step 5 â€” Troubleshooting
(a) Empty table â€” the poller is returning XML, or the HEC `Authorization` header is empty; set `Accept: application/json` and re-run. (b) `available=0` AND `used=0` â€” domain is inactive, detached, or the engine has not yet imported metadata; cross-check `status` in the API and the UI. (c) `external_status=unknown` everywhere â€” the engine is not in contact with the hosts; compare `ovirt:engine:host` for `up` count per cluster. (d) `over_sub_ratio` is always 1.0 â€” the domain is likely preallocated (thick) so committed tracks used; do not over interpret thin-oversubscription math. (e) `eta_to_critical_h` negative â€” the domain is gaining free space (deletions, snapshot merge); set to null in a follow-up `eval`. (f) 983/984 spam â€” the engine re-evaluates often; `dedup` in dashboards or a short throttle key on `storage_domain_id`. (g) Gluster â€” engine `available` can mask a bad brick; cross-check volume status. (h) iSCSI/FC flaps â€” require two consecutive `external_status=warning` samples or join an event. (i) Freeze tests â€” with `_hard=1` you should not see a demotion; if you do, the `_hard` expression failed to parse `error`. (j) RHV 4.4 vs oVirt 4.5 field names for `committed` / `commited` â€” add both spellings to `coalesce()`. (k) oVirt 4.6+ (Bella) â€” re-read the release note event codes and add them to the `where` in the `event` subsearch and poller as they appear; the poller and lookup pattern stays the same.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.36 â€” oVirt / RHV storage domain capacity: free %, low_space and critical_space watermarks, external_status, events 983/984. Multisearch ovirt:engine:storagedomain + host + event; lookup ovirt_storage_domain_owner; coalesce camel/snake; streamstats growth â†’ eta_to_critical_h; five-tier severity with freeze demotion that preserves critical.")`
| multisearch [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:storagedomain" earliest=-2h@h | comment("UC-19.2.36 â€” oVirt / RHV storage domain free %, watermarks, external_status, events 983+984. Primary: ovirt:engine:storagedomain. Then filter to storagedomain; events joined by storage_domain_id.") ] [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:host" earliest=-2h@h ] [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:event" earliest=-2h@h | eval _code=tonumber(toString(coalesce(code, eventCode, "")), 10) | where _code=983 OR _code=984 ]
| where sourcetype="ovirt:engine:storagedomain"
| eval data_center=toString(coalesce(data_center, dataCenter, data_center_name, dataCenterName, dc, ""))
| eval storage_domain_id=toString(coalesce(id, storage_domain_id, storageDomainId, "") )
| eval storage_domain_name=toString(coalesce(name, storage_domain_name, storageDomainName, ""))
| eval storage_type=toString(coalesce(storage_type, storageType, st_type, "unknown"))
| eval master=if((lower(toString(coalesce(master, isMaster, "false")))="true") OR (toString(coalesce(master, isMaster, "0"))="1"), 1, 0)
| eval external_status=lower(toString(coalesce(external_status, externalStatus, "unknown")))
| eval available=tonumber(toString(coalesce(available, availableSize, freeBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval used=tonumber(toString(coalesce(used, usedSize, usedBytes, "0")), 10)
| eval committed=tonumber(toString(coalesce(committed, committedSize,"0")), 10)
| eval warning_low_space_indicator=tonumber(toString(coalesce(warning_low_space_indicator, warningLowSpaceIndicator, "10")), 10)
| eval critical_space_action_blocker=tonumber(toString(coalesce(critical_space_action_blocker, criticalSpaceActionBlocker, "5")), 10)
| eval total_bytes=if((isnotnull(available)) AND (isnotnull(used)) AND ((available+used)>0), available+used, 0)
| eval free_pct=if((total_bytes>0), round(((available*1.0)/total_bytes)*100, 2), 0)
| eval available_gb=round(available/1073741824, 2)
| eval committed_gb=round(committed/1073741824, 2)
| eval over_sub_ratio=if((total_bytes>0), round((committed*1.0)/total_bytes, 3), 0)
| eval crit_bytes=if((isnotnull(critical_space_action_blocker)) AND (critical_space_action_blocker>0), critical_space_action_blocker*1073741824, 5368709120)
| sort 0 + _time, storage_domain_id
| streamstats current=f last(available) as avail_p last(_time) as t_p by storage_domain_id
| eval depl_bps=if((isnotnull(avail_p)) AND (isnotnull(t_p)) AND (_time>t_p) AND (avail_p>available), (avail_p-available)/(_time-t_p+0.0001), 0)
| eval eta_to_critical_h=if((depl_bps>0) AND (available>crit_bytes) AND (available>0), round(((available-crit_bytes)/depl_bps)/3600, 1), null())
| stats max(_time) as _time latest(data_center) as data_center latest(storage_domain_id) as storage_domain_id latest(storage_domain_name) as storage_domain_name latest(storage_type) as storage_type latest(master) as master latest(external_status) as external_status latest(available) as available latest(used) as used latest(committed) as committed latest(warning_low_space_indicator) as warning_low_space_indicator latest(critical_space_action_blocker) as critical_space_action_blocker latest(total_bytes) as total_bytes latest(free_pct) as free_pct latest(available_gb) as available_gb latest(committed_gb) as committed_gb latest(over_sub_ratio) as over_sub_ratio latest(eta_to_critical_h) as eta_to_critical_h latest(depl_bps) as depl_bps by data_center, storage_domain_id, storage_domain_name
| eval total_bytes=if((isnotnull(available)) AND (isnotnull(used)) AND ((available+used)>0), available+used, 0)
| eval free_pct=if((total_bytes>0), round(((available*1.0)/total_bytes)*100, 2), 0)
| eval available_gb=round(available/1073741824, 2)
| eval committed_gb=round(committed/1073741824, 2)
| eval over_sub_ratio=if((total_bytes>0), round((committed*1.0)/total_bytes, 3), 0)
| eval crit_bytes=if((isnotnull(critical_space_action_blocker)) AND (critical_space_action_blocker>0), critical_space_action_blocker*1073741824, 5368709120)
| lookup ovirt_storage_domain_owner data_center storage_domain_name OUTPUT min_free_pct, expected_critical_action_blocker_gb, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until, expansion_planned_date, criticality_class
| eval min_free_pct=tonumber(toString(min_free_pct), 10)
| eval expect_crit_gb=tonumber(toString(expected_critical_action_blocker_gb), 10)
| eval warning_low_space_indicator=if((isnotnull(min_free_pct)) AND (min_free_pct>0), min_free_pct, warning_low_space_indicator)
| eval critical_space_action_blocker=if((isnotnull(expect_crit_gb)) AND (expect_crit_gb>0), expect_crit_gb, critical_space_action_blocker)
| eval low_space_breach=if(free_pct<warning_low_space_indicator, 1, 0)
| eval critical_space_breach=if(available_gb<critical_space_action_blocker, 1, 0)
| join type=left storage_domain_id [ search index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine:event" earliest=-2h@h | eval _c=tonumber(toString(coalesce(code, eventCode, "")), 10) | where _c=983 OR _c=984 | eval storage_domain_id=toString(coalesce(related_storage_id, relatedStorageId, storage_domain_id, storageDomainId, storageId, id, "")) | stats max(if(_c=984,1,0)) as ev_984 max(if(_c=983,1,0)) as ev_983 by storage_domain_id ] | fillnull value=0 ev_984 ev_983
| eval recent_984=if(ev_984>0,1,0) | eval recent_983=if(ev_983>0,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case((external_status="error") OR (critical_space_breach=1) OR (recent_984=1), "critical", (low_space_breach=1) OR (external_status="warning") OR ((isnotnull(eta_to_critical_h)) AND (eta_to_critical_h<24) AND (depl_bps>0)) OR (recent_983=1), "high", (free_pct<25) OR (over_sub_ratio>2.0), "medium", (free_pct<40), "low", 1=1, "ok")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(ft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval _hard=(critical_space_breach=1) OR (external_status="error") OR (recent_984=1)
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1) AND (_hard=0) AND ((severity_bucket="high") OR (severity_bucket="medium") OR (severity_bucket="low")), "ok", severity_bucket)
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket="critical", 1, severity_bucket="high", 2, severity_bucket="medium", 3, severity_bucket="low", 4, 1=1, 5)
| sort 0 sev_order, -free_pct
| table data_center, storage_domain_name, storage_type, master, external_status, available_gb, total_bytes, free_pct, committed_gb, over_sub_ratio, warning_low_space_indicator, critical_space_action_blocker, low_space_breach, critical_space_breach, eta_to_critical_h, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, expansion_planned_date, criticality_class
```

## Visualization

Single value: count of `critical_space_breach=1` by `data_center`. Single value: count of `external_status=error`. Table: final `table` output sorted by `free_pct` ascending. Scatter: `over_sub_ratio` vs `free_pct` (colour by `severity_bucket`). Timechart: `max(free_pct)` or `min(available_gb)` over 7d with split by `storage_domain_name`. Sub-panel: `ovirt:engine:event` for codes 983/984 in the last 24h with `storage_domain_id` and message text.
